methods.description: This method describes the probabilistic model used to represent a climate variable of interest at specific coordinates and times. It involves using a Gaussian Process (GP) with specific spatial and temporal kernels to model the climate variable and its posterior distribution.

steps.description: Adopt a zero-mean, factorized GP prior for C(x, t) := C(x, t) − m(x) using a Matérn-3/2 spatial kernel and a Matérn-1/2 temporal kernel.
steps.searchable_summary: Define GP prior with Matérn kernels for climate variable

steps.description: Define the likelihood for site-specific reconstruction or simulated value Y s,p, assuming i.i.d. normal distribution.
steps.searchable_summary: Define likelihood for site-specific data assuming i.i.d. normal distribution

steps.description: Use the same σ for all simulations and pollen-based reconstructions.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply uniform σ across simulations and reconstructions

steps.description: Query the resulting GP posterior distribution of C at any spatiotemporal point (x, t).
steps.searchable_summary: Query GP posterior distribution at spatiotemporal points

steps.description: Use a sparse GP approximation for inference due to the intractability of O(N^3) inversion of the kernel Gram matrix.
steps.searchable_summary: Implement sparse GP approximation for inference
